description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a long, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain and a smooth finish. The handle is ergonomically shaped, tapering towards the end for a secure grip, and appears to be made from a single piece of hardwood. The axe head is forged from metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end. The blade has a straight cutting edge and a pronounced beard, allowing for controlled chopping and splitting. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a classic wedged fitting, with no visible screws or bolts. The overall appearance suggests a robust, utilitarian tool suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks in urban or suburban environments.
description: A traditional axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, wedge-shaped metal blade, designed for chopping and splitting tasks.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, flat profile of the metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the width of the blade and the handle's ergonomic curvature.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's length and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view emphasizing the handle's end and the thickness of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth, ergonomic grip and oval cross-section; natural wood grain visible.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a straight edge and pronounced beard; flat poll on the opposite side.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
